transcript: The film begins with a scene at the Mexican border, where gang members are doing illegal drug deals. At that time, a group of masked individuals storms in and holds them at gunpoint. One of the gang members tries to convince the masked people that he and his friend are actually undercover DEA agents, but they don't believe him. The masked group ends up killing everyone in the building except for two women who witnessed the incident and tried to escape. The masked people chase after them, but the sound of police sirens makes them flee. Then, the story shifts to America, where an old detective named Johansson is getting ready for work while watching news about a protest at the police station. The protest is by Mexican migrants upset about a police officer's brutal attack on a Mexican driver. When Johansen arrives at the office, he's confronted by police chief Hernandez, who shows him a video of Johansen attacking the Mexican driver. This violence is seen as disrespectful. Johansen also made a derogatory comment about Mexicans, which adds to his troubles. Because of his actions, Johansson is at risk of losing his job and pension, and he might face legal consequences. To redeem himself, Hernandez assigns him a special task to retrieve two women who witnessed the murder of DEA agents in Mexico. These women are now in a Mexican prison. At that time, Johansson has no choice but to accept the assignment. In the evening, Johansson went to a bar with his friend Hiltz and another retired cop named Brinner. There, Johansson complained about how he felt frustrated with Mexicans coming into America illegally and getting things for free. Hiltz and Brenner suggested Johansson retire and not go to Mexico. Despite their advice, Johansson still chose to go. As he was leaving the bar, a Mexican man accidentally bumped into Johansson's car, which made him angry, but he managed to keep his cool this time. The next day, Johansson headed to Mexico. When he arrived at the police station, he had to give up his gun and was greeted by a Mexican cop named De La Cruz. Cruz then took Johansson to get the two witnesses from prison. Three hours later, they reached the women's prison. Leticia and Rosa, the two prisoners, were handed over to Johansson. Leticia got scared because someone had tried to kill them before, but Johansson didn't take it seriously. As they were driving, a police car started following them. Cruz, feeling like he was being chased by the cops, refused to stop. Suddenly, one of the policemen fired shots, causing chaos. Johansson managed to take down the two mysterious cops, but got shot in the process. He quickly called Hernandez to update him and realized he was injured. Sadly, both Cruz and Leticia didn't make it. At that time, Johansson pleaded with Rosa for help, but she initially wanted to leave him. Eventually, seeing Johansson getting weaker, Rosa decided to assist him. When Johansson's phone rang, Rosa grabbed it, thinking it might reveal their location to their enemies. Amidst the chaos, Johansson passed out. The next day, he woke up in treatment, with his hands cuffed to the bed. Later, Rosa entered with her older brother, Miguel, who is a Mexican policeman. She explained that the two cops who attacked them were real, but in Mexico, badges don't show which side someone's on. Meanwhile, Brinner met Hiltz at the police station after hearing about Johansson shooting Mexican police and fleeing with a witness. Later, in Hernandez's room, Brinner expressed his wish to help find Johansson, even though he was retired. Since Brinner was Johansson's friend, Hernandez politely declined his offer. In Mexico, Rosa told Johansson that the person who killed the DEA member was an American cop. She overheard one of the masked men saying he was working with the DEA agent they attacked. This explained why the agent recognized one of the masked men. Hearing this, Johansson asked to make a call, because Rosa and her family could be in danger the longer they waited. Johansson promised Rosa witness protection if she shared what she knew. Soon after, Rosa gave him her phone, and he immediately contacted Hernandez for help. Hernandez was surprised to hear about the DEA agent's shooting and agreed to meet Johansson and Rosa at the American consulate. During their chat, Miguel interrupted to take Johansson and Rosa to lunch. He thanked Johansson for saving Rosa from two corrupt policemen hired by the cartel to kill them. But Johansson got upset when Miguel admitted he had to handcuff him for their safety because Johansson was accused of killing a federal police officer and two Mexican cops. Despite Johansson's explanation that he was only there to ensure Rosa's safety, Miguel had to call his brother Salvador to handcuff Johansson again. After meals, Rosa pleaded with Miguel to let her go to America with Johansson, but Miguel refused. Suddenly, they heard their dog barking, and Miguel saw armed men surrounding their house, demanding Rosa and Johansson be handed over. Miguel told Rosa to take their mom to safety while he and Salvador dealt with the attackers. In the chaos, Miguel gave Salvador the handcuff key to release Johansson. Sadly, Salvador got shot as soon as he entered the room. With one hand still cuffed, Johansson managed to take down Salvador's attacker. Soon after, Johansson hurried to unlock the handcuffs and managed to save the injured Miguel just in time. He also acted quickly to stop a man from harming Rosa and her mother. Unfortunately, he couldn't catch another man who was fleeing. After the attack, Miguel finally agreed to let Johansson leave with Rosa. Rosa's mother gave her a lot of money for their journey, and before they left, she gave Johansson a necklace, telling him in Spanish to fight like a wolf if he wants to defeat a wolf. On their way, Johansen planned to call Hernandez again, but Rosa reminded him angrily that every time he did, someone tried to kill them. She suggested contacting a trusted person to pick them up at the border. Rosa found someone to smuggle them across the border the next night and paid him well with the money for her mother. That night, Johansson and Rosa stayed at a hotel, where Johansson contacted his only trusted friend, Brinner. The next morning, Johansson and Rosa were startled awake by a knock on their door. It was Hiltz, who had found them easily because there were only three hotels nearby. He came with Brinner and another friend. Hiltz warned them that the police and FBI were planning to come that day and suggested they go with them instead of waiting for the smuggler that night. Since they were good friends, Johansson agreed. Shortly after, they left in separate cars, with Johansson and Rosa riding with their friends Tinelli and Brinner, while Hiltz drove his own car. Brinner chose a different route, saying he had contacts to help them cross the border safely. Along the way, Rosa noticed a bracelet on Brinner's wrist, just like the one worn by a masked man during the incident. Remembering her mother's words, Rosa whispered to Johansson that their friends might not be trustworthy. They realized Hiltz wasn't alone. He had another friend named Leo in his car. Worried, Johansson asked Brinner to stop so they could talk. Outside, Johansson mentioned that the person who killed the DEA agent might be a cop. He pretended to ask Brinner who he thought did it. When Tinelli and Hiltz approached, Brinner and Tinelli pulled out their guns and forced Johansson to hand over his weapon. Amid the tension, Hiltz explained they only wanted the money and to fight the Mexican drug cartels. They didn't know about the DEA that night. Hiltz even tried to stop Johansson from going to Mexico to avoid trouble. Then Brinner ordered Tinelli to take Rosa out, but Hiltz disagreed with threatening their own friends. He tried to grab the gun, causing Brinner to accidentally shoot. This gave Johansson a chance to fight back, and he managed to defeat Tinelli, Brinner, and Leo. Shortly after, Johansson approached Hiltz, who was badly hurt, but sadly, Hiltz didn't make it. After that, Johansson and Rosa decided to go back to the city and stick to their plan with the smugglers. That night, they crossed the border with other people seeking refuge. The next day, they still had to walk several kilometers until they reached American soil. During this time, Johansen's view on Mexican migrants changed. He realized they were willing to risk a lot to come to America for a better life, rather than just wanting freebies. But just as they were getting excited, American police showed up and arrested them. A month later, Hernandez praised Johansen for his unexpected success. By exposing corrupt cops, he had cleaned up the police department. Later, Rosa, looking elegant, told Johansson she had been offered American citizenship by the prosecutor. While Johansson was happy for her, he also knew their time together was ending. In the end, Johansson, realizing it might be their last chance, asked Rosa out on a date. The film ends. The moral lesson from this film is never judge a taco by its shell. Just because someone bumps into your car doesn't mean they're not your enemy. We'll see you next time.
